questions.question: What is the value of the digit 5 in the number $456,789$?
A hiker travels $234.56$ miles in a day. Rounded to the nearest ten, how many miles did the hiker travel?
A wildlife reserve has $14,567$ animals. Write this number in expanded form.
What is the standard form of the number $2 \cdot 10^3 + 5 \cdot 10^2 + 6 \cdot 10^1 + 8 \cdot 10^0$?
A park ranger measures the length of a trail to be $4,321.9$ meters. Rounded to the nearest hundred, what is the length of the trail?
What is the value of the digit 9 in the number $987,654$?
A group of friends plan to drive $278.5$ miles to a campsite. If their car gets $25$ miles per gallon, how many gallons of fuel will they use, rounded to the nearest tenth?
A conservation team counts $3,456$ animals in a wildlife sanctuary. Write this number in standard form.
A group of hikers travel $145.23$ miles in a day. Write this number in expanded form and round it to the nearest ten. Show your work and explain your reasoning.
A park has $6,789$ visitors in a month. Write this number in standard form and round it to the nearest hundred. Explain the difference between the original number and the rounded number.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand place value before moving on to more complex topics
Tip 2: Encourage students to use visual aids such as number lines and blocks to help with rounding and expanded form
Tip 3: Remind students to always check their work and explain their reasoning
